text.title: Specification Writing Checklist
text.summary: Phase-by-phase checklist from context preparation through stakeholder sign-off.

list.item: Define the problem statement: what problem are we solving and why does it matter
Identify target users and their personas
List known constraints: technical, business, regulatory, timeline
Gather existing context: related specs, codebase patterns, past decisions
Prepare domain terminology the AI needs to understand
Set scope boundaries: what is explicitly NOT in scope
Choose appropriate model: Claude for complexity, GPT for versatility, Gemini for scale
Describe the feature or system in natural language
Let AI ask clarifying questions — answer all questions thoroughly
Identify stakeholders: who cares about this, who approves
Define success criteria: how do we know when it's done
Document assumptions: what are we taking for granted
Review AI's understanding: does it match your mental model
Correct any misunderstandings explicitly
Generate functional requirements list (FR-1, FR-2, ... format)
Verify completeness: ask AI "what functionality might I have missed"
Prioritize: MoSCoW (must-have vs nice-to-have)
Check for conflicts: ask AI to identify conflicting requirements
Generate non-functional requirements: performance, security, scalability, reliability
Ask AI to enumerate edge cases: "what could go wrong"
Document error scenarios, boundary conditions, concurrent scenarios
Write at least one Given-When-Then criterion per functional requirement
Include both positive (happy path) and negative (error path) scenarios
Use concrete data values — not "valid input" but "user@example.com"
Verify each criterion is independently testable without ambiguity
Check: no vague terms like "quickly", "efficiently", "properly"
Ask AI to review for missing requirements
Ask AI to identify conflicting requirements
Ask AI to flag ambiguous acceptance criteria
Check: all requirements have acceptance criteria
Check: all edge cases documented and handled
Check: non-goals clearly stated
Check: dependencies explicitly listed
Verify business logic is correct against actual domain knowledge
Confirm edge cases are realistic — not over-engineered
Verify constraints are accurately stated
Product owner approval: requirements match vision
Tech lead approval: design is feasible
QA approval: criteria are testable
Commit spec to git: version-control every iteration
